title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After discovering a woman's body in a fur coat buried within a stone coffin in a warehouse near Rutherford Hall, Lucy drives to a post office to call Miss Marple. Lucy informs Miss Marple of the discovery in the antique-filled warehouse and suggests calling the police. Miss Marple advises Lucy to tell the truth to the authorities. Lucy then calls the police station to report the body found at Rutherford Hall.

Returning to Rutherford Manor, Lucy seeks out Emma to inform her of the discovery privately, wanting to avoid upsetting Emma's sick father, Mr. Crickenshaw. Emma is stunned by the news and expresses frustration that Lucy did not inform her before calling the police. Lucy explains she used the post office phone to avoid being overheard by a boy.

The police arrive, led by Bacon. Emma, having called Dr. Camper to attend to her father, is escorted out of the warehouse. Dr. Camper, an affable and cynical physician, observes Emma and comments on her character and her instinct to care for the men in her family. Bacon informs the doctor that the coroner, Johnstone, estimates the woman was killed two to three weeks ago. Despite the unpleasant smell, Dr. Camper expresses curiosity about the victim's identity. Mr. Crickenshaw, who purchased the stone coffin in Florence years prior, joins the group, insisting on fulfilling his duty to see the body despite the repulsive scene.
